oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Bring value to clients: Investing in a systematic approach to evaluating calf programs
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Reed, Kelly
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: calf management
disease investigation
calf protocols
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This discussion will walk clinicians through supporting calf programs, covering the opportunity to be consistently involved in calf program protocols as well as systematically evaluating disease challenges when they occur. Topics will include the value of getting involved, observations to make when walking calves and evaluating critical control points, colostrum man­agement and dry cow care, cleanliness and sanitation, consis­tent delivery of milk, nutrition, environment and growth goals. This will include basic tools to utilize and guidelines for key pa­rameters for success. Many farms are underserved in the moni­toring of their calf rearing programs. Given the current value of calves, this is an opportunity for veterinarians to extend their services and their relationship with their client. This discussion should be beneficial to practitioners regardless of level of ex­pertise in calf health and management as the focus is practical management considerations.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 55-58
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
